title: MoonLeaf Reader
content:
I stopped reading books because of my phone.
Not because I got lazy. Because every reading app felt like it was fighting me.
Ads between chapters. Eye-burning white screens at midnight. Pages that scroll like a Twitter feed — not like a book.
So I built my own.
Moonleaf — Read by moonlight.
A distraction-free book reader, built from scratch with Flutter.


Here's what makes it different:


📖 Real paper-curl page turns. Not a library animation. I wrote a custom rendering engine — 744 lines of CustomPainter math — that snapshots pages to bitmap, computes fold geometry, diagonal shadows, mirrored back-faces, and highlights, all on the GPU canvas. Zero widget rebuilds. 60fps butter.


🎨 Three reading palettes: Light, Sepia, Night. Independent of the app theme. Because your reading surface isn't your settings screen. A warmth slider blends the background toward amber — so "Night" mode actually feels like reading by candlelight, not staring at a void.


📄 Import your own PDFs. Layout, fonts, images — everything preserved. But the surface underneath adapts. Sepia mode multiplies a warm cream onto white pages. Night mode inverts luminance with a raw color matrix. Your PDF. Moonleaf's soul.


✨ Text highlighting with color pickers. Select text, choose from 5 colours — Yellow, Green, Blue, Pink, Orange — highlights persist across sessions with normalised coordinates so they're resolution-independent.


📚 Collections. Organise your imported PDFs into folders. Create them inline. Move books between collections. Simple, like it should be.


🔖 "Continue Reading" spotlight. Opens the app → your last book is right there, with the chapter name, cover gradient, and one-tap to resume. No hunting.
The architecture underneath?


MVVM + SOLID. 57 Dart files. ~8,500 lines. Every ViewModel talks to service interfaces, not implementations. All wiring happens in one service_locator.dart. The kind of codebase where you can swap out SharedPreferences for Hive in 10 minutes and nothing else notices.
The fonts are bundled. Lora for reading. Inter for UI. No network calls for typography. Ever.
I didn't build Moonleaf to compete with Kindle.
I built it because I wanted to enjoy reading on my phone again.
And then I open-sourced it — because maybe you do too.

🔗 GitHub → https://github.com/tamilarasu18/moonleaf-reader
MIT Licensed. Fork it. Break it. Make it yours.

#Flutter #OpenSource #MobileApp #Reading #IndieHacker #BuildInPublic #DartLang #MVVM #CleanArchitecture #Moonleaf #SideProject #AppDev #flutterApp
